reportText: @ Canyon Mouth , 7 cfs @ Fort Collins , 1 cfs @ North Fork below Seaman Reservoir The warmer, spring-like weather this week has started pushing snowmelt down the canyon. Flows are on the rise, and the water is a bit off-color. That said, fishing has still been great! We’ve been seeing some small Blue Winged Olives in the canyon, and dare we say it looks like an early, light terrestrial bite is starting to show up. Consider running a dry-dropper rig in the afternoons. With temps climbing on the Poudre, bug diversity is improving. Stick with smaller midges and Blue Wings, but don’t hesitate to mix in a smaller stonefly imitation. With the off-color water, attractor patterns and brighter flies have been producing well. Emergers are still a key player and will keep you in the game. Spring is definitely starting to show its face around here, and we’re getting more guide trips out on the Poudre. Give us a call to get the lowdown! Take a look at our Poudre Assortment , a great selection for this time of year.
